_note: ORIGINAL content. Think C++ and the Google C++ Class outline informed coverage only; no prose, examples, or exercises were copied.
title: Loops
summary: Repeat statements with a clear exit condition.
refresher: A `for` loop groups initialization, condition, and update. A `while` loop repeats while its condition is true. Trace the first value, final included value, and update.

blocks.body: A `for` loop groups initialization, condition, and update. A `while` loop repeats while its condition is true. Trace the first value, final included value, and update. Repeat statements with a clear exit condition.

Trace a loop as initialization, test, body, and update. For container traversal, the first index is zero and the condition should stay strictly below the size. A range-based loop avoids manual indexes when you only need each element value.

blocks.caption: The counter begins at zero and stops when it reaches three.
Use a strict bound so the count is never used as an index.

blocks.body: For three elements, valid indexes are zero, one, and two. Once i becomes three, the corrected condition fails. The inclusive version allows three and accesses beyond the vector. If no index is needed, `for (int value : values)` is simpler.

blocks.title: Follow the data
blocks.body: A while loop whose tested variable never changes can run forever; identify the exact statement responsible for reaching the exit.

blocks.why: The loop includes one through four because its condition includes the upper boundary.
Count stays equal to three, leaving the while condition true forever.
